name: Akorion EzyAgric App
description: EzyAgric is an app-based platform for digitizing agricultural value chains to enable all commercial farmers and other agribusinesses to access production and marketing services.

taxonomy.sub_category: Akorion EzyAgric App Updated on June 11, 2024 · Created on October 8, 2018 Akorion EzyAgric App EzyAgric is an app-based platform for digitizing agricultural value chains to enable all commercial farmers and other agribusinesses to access production and marketing services. Developed By Akorion Tested By Akorion EzyAgric App Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description EzyAgric is an app-based platform for digitizing agricultural value chains to enable all commercial farmers and other agribusinesses to access production and marketing services. Target SDGs SDG 2: Zero Hunger SDG 1: No Poverty Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Implementer: Akorion Partners: RCMRD, USAID, SG2000, NARO, Star Café, Savannah Commodities, Pearl Seeds, Bukoola, Balton Uganda, NASECO, Grain Pulse, UAP, Letshego, Opportunity Bank and World Food Programme Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include M-Farm , 6th Grain , WeFarm , and Esoko . Countries Uganda Manufacturing/Building Method N/A Intellectural Property Type Copyright User Provision Model e-VAM (Electronic Village Agent Model): a service delivery model in which community-based service providers equipped with smartphones deliver services on demand to the farmers. App downloaded from Google Play store. Farmers digital profiling done by 480 village agents and 100 farmer associations. Distributions to Date Status 106,018 farmers served Design Specifications Features: AgricShop, Farm Records & Analysis, EzyCredits, Garden Mapping, MArket Linkage, Farm Manager, Extension and Advisory, Produce Markets Technical Support Provided by Akorion Replacement Components N/A Lifecycle N/A Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters For farmers: Prediction of demand and supply and access to the market Informed decision-making in production and marketing through seasonal Profit & Loss – Income and Expenses analysis Traceability of farmer groups member’s activities by farmers, service providers (village agents) and association staff For suppliers: Prediction of demand and supply efficiently and reduce losses due to poor planning Information about market dynamics and conditions in Agriculture For financial institutions: Reduced costs of credit assessment and loan appraisal plus monitoring Access to clients Monitoring and traceability of farmers for better performance and decision making For donors and governments: Informed decisions in planning, strategy building and implementation of initiatives through accurate statistics of the agricultural activities in specific areas. Access to real time reports and analysis of trends in Agriculture and ICT in Agriculture Monitoring and traceability of beneficiaries. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety N/A Complementary Technical Systems N/A Academic Research and References Hilary, R. S., Sseguya, H., & Kibwika, P. (2017). Information quality, sharing and usage in farmer organizations: The case of rice value chains in Bugiri and Luwero Districts, Uganda . Cogent Food & Agriculture, 3(1), 1350089. “EzyAgric Farmer.” n.d. Google.Com. Accessed June 11, 2024. https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.ezyagric.extension.android&hl=en “THE 17 GOALS.” n.d. Sdgs.Un.Org. Accessed June 11, 2024. https://sdgs.un.org/goals “EzyAgric.” n.d. Ezyagric.Com.
